excerpt: When the time comes to establish the da'wah, it is obligatory for the mukasar to do something to the person who is ignorant of this religion in a way that will shake the soul of that ignorant person and he will wake up from his dream of negligence and move forward with a passion towards the call to truth.
One should not urinate in the holes in the earth because there are living creatures such as snakes etc—creatures of the earth (ḥashārāt al-arḍ).
Allah will gather my Umma on the Day of Resurrection among the nations, their foreheads gleaming and their limbs radiant from the marks of ablution (min āthār al-wuḍūʾ).

When I was ascended to the heavens, I beheld certain angels in dispute one with another concerning three matters: the washing of ablution in times of hardship, the hastening of footsteps to join the congregation, and the waiting for prayer after prayer.

The Messenger of Allah was sent as a mercy not alone to mankind but to all worlds and creatures. The water of his ablution possessed within it the essence of the most pure spiritual realities, and through such grace, a human being fallen into the form of a cat was transformed and elevated.
source_work: Taʾwīl al-Daʿāʾim (Esoteric Exegesis of the Pillars of Faith)
source_author: Attributed to Ismāʿīl ibn Hibat Allāh
source_location_hint: Tradition of the Prophet and the Cat
